title: Easy Wall Mail or Magazine Bin Shelf
summary: A wall-mounted sorter with three angled bins for mail or magazines, built from a single 1x2 and one piece of 1/4" plywood — a true scrap-bin project.
description:
This little organizer tames the pile of mail by the door: a 1x2 frame holding three angled 1/4" plywood bins, mountable on a wall. It uses almost nothing — one 10-foot 1x2 and an off-the-shelf 24"x48" sheet of thin plywood or hardboard — so it's an ideal use for scraps and a genuinely quick build.

The only cut worth thinking about is the angled bin sides. The tip in the plan is the clean way to do it: rip a strip of plywood 7-1/4" wide and at least 16" long, set your saw to a 25° angle, and crosscut the bin sides off it, leaving 3/4" on the short end. That 25° is what tips the bins back so mail sits in them instead of falling out. Batch-cutting them off one strip keeps all six identical.

Assembly is forgiving. Build the 1x2 frame with pocket holes, and use the bin sides themselves as spacers to set the middle rails so you don't have to measure. Staple or nail the bin sides flush to the back, then glue the front panels on with cyanoacrylate (Krazy) glue and let it fully cure before moving the piece. Add a plywood back if you want it fully enclosed.

tools.note: For ripping the plywood strips cleanly; a circular saw and straightedge also work.
Or a staple gun for the bin sides.

materials.name: 1/4" plywood or hardboard, 24" x 48"
materials.note: Bin sides and fronts. Sold this size off the shelf.

cutList.note: Cut at 25° leaving 3/4" on the short end — rip a 7-1/4" strip and crosscut them off it.

steps.title: Build the frame
steps.body: Build the 1x2 frame with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" screws. Use the bin sides themselves as spacers to set the middle rails, so you don't have to measure the spacing.

steps.title: Attach the bin sides
steps.body: Cut the angled bin sides (25°, 3/4" short end) and glue-and-nail or staple them to the frame, flush to the back, with 3/4" or 1" fasteners. The angle tips each bin back so contents stay put.

steps.title: Glue on the fronts and finish
steps.body: Glue the bin fronts on with cyanoacrylate glue and let it fully cure before moving the piece. Add a 1/4" plywood back with glue and short nails if you want it enclosed. Finish and hang on the wall.

images.alt: Wall-mounted mail sorter with three angled bins
